items.title: Court sets aside convictions of parents for starving daughter, grants bail

children.content: A Perth man and woman who were found guilty in 2024 of starving their daughter, who weighed about 28 kilograms when she was admitted to hospital aged nearly 17, have their convictions set aside on appeal.

gallery.altText: A digitally produced image featuring a man and a woman covering their heads with jackets, and a slightly built girl.
A man and woman walking down the street, covering their heads with jackets
Three women with blurred faces walk down stairs outside a court. One is young and two are older.

A man and woman walking down the street, covering their heads with jackets
Three women with blurred faces walk down stairs outside a court. One is young and two are older.

items.title: Driver 'didn't have time to brake' before crash that killed boy on scooter

children.content: An 11-year-old boy dies after his scooter collides with a car in Mandurah, with a witness saying they saw the boy riding down a small hill onto the road before the crash — WA's fourth road fatality over the Easter period.

image.altText: Flowers and a teddy bear sit on a limestone wall.

items.title: Claims wild-caught Australian seafood will soon 'only be afforded by the rich'

children.content: Australian seafood prices are likely to rise due to fuel supply issues, with some warning that wild-caught seafood could become unaffordable for most.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith's fight against war crimes allegations just got more perilous

children.content: During his years-long battle to clear his name, a separate joint investigation stretching 9,000 kilometres was discreetly transpiring.

gallery.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

thumbnail.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

items.title: Having seen prisons from the inside, Zach says they're just 'breeding criminals'

children.content: Hundreds of legal, health and children's organisations are calling on Anthony Albanese to convene an emergency summit to address the over-incarceration of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children.

image.altText: A young man stands in front of an Aboriginal flag wearing a grey hoodie with his hands outclasped together in front of his face.

items.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death

children.content: Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time about the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, saying he and his children have since been "harassed" and "stalked" by the media.

image.altText: Rohan Dennis wearing formal attire walks along a sandstone wall, with a broadcast camera partially visible.

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

gallery.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.
A man wearing a dark polo shirt sits on a park bench with his back turned to the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.
A man wearing a dark polo shirt sits on a park bench with his back turned to the camera.

items.title: 'I just had to do it for me': The ambiguous loss of family estrangement

children.content: Estrangement usually begins after a significant traumatic experience with a loved one, experts say.

image.altText: A man sitting on a rock looking out towards the ocean

items.title: Owner of dog that mauled young girl charged over attack

children.content: The owner of a dog that savaged a girl in a Perth suburb, leaving her with serious facial injuries, is charged under Western Australia's Dog Act.

image.altText: A residential street in the Perth suburb of Hillarys on a sunny day, with houses on both sides of the road.

items.title: Tourists urged not to cancel plans as Cyclone Maila intensifies

children.content: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila is now a category five system and is expected to cross into Far North Queensland on Sunday.

image.altText: computer image of cyclone off queensland coast

items.title: Team behind cult 2012 video game reunites on Grammy winner

children.content: Art director Matt Nava and composer Austin Wintory reveal how their cult 2012 video game Journey shaped their latest offering, the Grammy Award-winning Sword of the Sea.

image.altText: A video game character grinds their board along a large metal chain in what appears to be an underwater ruined city

items.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches

children.content: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears Cyclone Narelle might be a climate disaster that results in a "double whammy" for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

gallery.altText: A dead dolphin on a beach, with debris washed up behind it.
A dead turtle hatchling is held aloft, still in its broken shell.
An aerial image of a medium-sized boat floating on a blue-green reef with snorkelers in the foreground.

items.title: Too much of not enough: Sport stars consider leaving over paltry pay

children.content: Recently retired rugby union player Mel Kawa regularly broke down in tears, grappling with her love of the game and the $2,000 she was paid to play it.

image.altText: Melanie Kawa sits on a bench seat and holds a football.

items.title: Why these dogs are on the pool deck at Australian Open swimming titles

children.content: The swimmers at the Australian Open championships this week have had additional support from the side of the pool in the form of three retriever dogs.

image.altText: A swimmer in race gear smiles and pats a black dog.

items.title: 'No one else should go through this': Young job hunters a target in scam surge

children.content: Job seekers should be wary of sharing their bank account information with potential employers, as job scams targeting young people surge, according to Scamwatch.

gallery.altText: A man is scrolling on his phone.

thumbnail.altText: A man is scrolling on his phone.

items.title: Quiz: Pack your suitcase, we're going on a trip

children.content: Which European microstate is smaller? And which US president isn’t on Mount Rushmore?

image.altText: Elvis Presley impersonators gather for the Guinness World Record for the largest gathering of Elvis Presley impersonators.

items.title: Matt Canavan looks to stars for 'Australia on steroids' plan

children.content: A domestic space industry, a baby boom, new dams and more factories are on Nationals leader Matt Canavan's to-do list to deliver what he describes as an Australian economic revolution.

image.altText: Matt Canavan, smiling and wearing a suit and tie, walks down a corridor at parliament house.

items.title: Blues recruit's striking ban upheld by AFL tribunal

children.content: Carlton will be without Will Hayward for a high-stakes clash against Adelaide after failing to downgrade his one-match ban for striking.

image.altText: AFL players come together in a scuffle on the field, pulling jerseys and pushing

items.title: Calls to force NDIS providers to register as Labor eyes savings

children.content: Stricter registration rules would weed out low-quality NDIS providers while delivering better services and budget savings, major operators say.

image.altText: two adults and a child on a bike in front of a river

items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers in Perth reporting a surge in sales.

articles.title: All WA regional stations must report fuel prices from May as FuelWatch scheme expands

children.content: The FuelWatch reporting scheme is being expanded to all regional fuel stations in Western Australia, with about 200 additional retailers required to report their fuel prices.

image.altText: Fuel pumps and screens depicting prices for petrol and diesal at a service station.

A sign on the ground outside a bike shop reading "beat the fuel prices, e-bike sale now on".
